evals.label: run evals when evals exist
evals.expected_output: The assistant detects existing evals.json and runs `npx snapeval eval` without going through any interactive setup phases.
assertions: The response runs or references running `npx snapeval eval`.
The response does not go through an interactive setup or scenario design flow.
The response interprets benchmark results or pass rates in plain language.
The response mentions the delta between with-skill and without-skill.

evals.label: re-eval after skill changes
evals.prompt: I just updated my SKILL.md for `skills/release-notes`. Run evals again and tell me if it improved.
evals.expected_output: The assistant runs evals, compares with previous iteration, and reports which evals improved or regressed.
assertions: The response runs `npx snapeval eval` to create a new iteration.
The response compares the new iteration with the previous one.
The response shows per-eval changes or a net delta.
The response gives a clear verdict on whether the changes improved or regressed.

evals.label: hand off when no evals exist
evals.prompt: Evaluate `skills/new-skill` — it has no evals yet.
evals.expected_output: The assistant detects no evals.json and hands off to snapeval-setup.
assertions: The response indicates no evals exist for this skill.
The response offers to help set up evals or mentions the setup flow.
The response does not attempt to run `npx snapeval eval` when no evals.json exists.

evals.label: add a new eval case
evals.prompt: Add an eval for empty input to my `skills/greeter` skill.
evals.expected_output: The assistant reads the existing evals.json, adds a new eval with the next available ID, preserves existing evals, and runs just the new one.
assertions: The response reads the existing evals/evals.json first.
The response adds a new eval with the next available ID.
The response preserves all existing evals without renumbering.
The response uses --only to run just the new eval.

evals.label: review results with patterns
evals.expected_output: The assistant runs review, interprets pass rates, surfaces patterns like always-pass/always-fail assertions, and suggests improvements.
assertions: The response runs or references `npx snapeval eval` or `npx snapeval review`.
The response interprets results in plain language.
The response surfaces patterns such as always-pass, always-fail, or differentiating assertions.
The response suggests concrete improvements based on what failed.
